title: RNA-seq Cell-Type Classification (PWDR)
sub_domain: Single-cell / bulk RNA-seq transcript quantification with marker-gene-panel cell-type categorical readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 physical_with_discrete_readout (PWDR) genesis Principle. Wraps L1-413 HMM Sequence Alignment (existing in v1 genesis, sibling computational-biology core; note imperfect fit because RNA-seq quantification is more accurately a Bayesian / EM transcript-abundance recovery — a future dedicated RNA-seq quantification analytical core could be authored). Threshold function applies canonical marker-gene-panel rules (CellMarker, PanglaoDB) to assign cell-type labels. Universal in single-cell biology, immunology, oncology, developmental biology. UTSW Children's Medical Center Research Institute and Simmons Cancer Center single-cell programs.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on cell-type categorical readout per CellMarker / PanglaoDB taxonomy

discrete_readout.threshold_function: Cell-type(cell) = argmax_class score_class where score_class = product over marker gene g in panel_class of [I(expression(g) > tau_marker_g)] OR weighted sum of expression z-scores. Marker-gene panels per CellMarker (Zhang 2019), PanglaoDB (Franzen 2019), or Tabula Sapiens (Tabula Sapiens Consortium 2022). Examples: T-cell = {CD3D, CD3E, CD3G high, CD19 low}; B-cell = {CD19, MS4A1, CD79A high, CD3 low}; macrophage = {CD68, CD14, ITGAM high}; epithelial-basal = {KRT5, KRT14, TP63 high}.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: per-cell normalized gene expression vector (typically log2 counts-per-million or scTransform-residual values across ~20k transcripts) recovered by L1-413-style alignment + transcript quantification
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: Marker-gene panel scoring is a sum of indicator functions on continuous-valued expression z-scores; per-class boundaries form measure-zero hyperplanes in expression space. Smoothed-classification (softmax with finite temperature) is globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.05 per z-score unit.

related_principles: L1-413 (HMM Sequence Alignment — sibling computational-biology core, physics_core_principle; note imperfect fit, RNA-seq quantification is a Bayesian / EM transcript-abundance problem)
L1-412 (protein folding — sibling computational biology, informational)

E.description: RNA-seq Cell-Type Classification (PWDR): wraps RNA-seq alignment + transcript quantification core with canonical marker-gene-panel rules. Stage 1 (analytical, sibling to L1-413): align reads to transcriptome (HISAT2, STAR, kallisto, Salmon); estimate per-transcript abundance via EM (RSEM, Kallisto pseudoalignment) or Bayesian inference (Salmon variational); normalize to counts-per-million or apply scTransform/sctransform Pearson residuals. Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): per-cell argmax over marker-panel scores per CellMarker / PanglaoDB / Tabula Sapiens taxonomies. Difficulty tier delta = 5. Mismatch parameters: dropout_rate, batch_effect, doublet_contamination, ambient_rna_contamination, marker_panel_coverage_uncertainty, taxonomy_disagreement.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (sibling to L1-413): reads -> transcriptome alignment -> counts -> log2 CPM or scTransform residuals across ~20k genes per cell. Stage 2: cell_type = argmax_class score(expression vector, marker panel_class) per CellMarker/PanglaoDB.
E.world_state_x: Per-cell categorical label in canonical cell-type taxonomy (immune, epithelial, stromal, neural sub-classes).
E.observation_y: Single-cell or bulk RNA-seq read library: typically 10x Genomics 3' or 5' chemistry, 50k-200k reads per cell, ~20k expressed transcripts.

G.dag: L.poly_a_capture -> L.reverse_transcription -> L.pcr_amplification -> L.sequencing -> L.transcriptome_alignment -> L.transcript_quantification -> L.normalization -> L.marker_panel_classifier -> int.cell
coupling_constraints: transcriptome_to_classifier: gene-expression vector and marker-panel reference share gene-identifier indexing; coupling enables per-cell argmax classification

W.regime: Existence guaranteed within Omega bounds. Uniqueness conditional on adequate sequencing depth (typically >30k reads per cell for 3' chemistry) and adequate marker-panel coverage. Stability conditional with dropout_rate dominant for low-expressing markers; batch_effect dominant cross-sample; doublet_contamination dominant for high cell densities. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled RNA-seq + marker-panel-classifier forward established by Trapnell 2014 (foundational scRNA-seq), Macosko 2015 (Drop-seq), Stuart-Butler 2019 (Seurat v3 integration), Tabula Sapiens Consortium 2022, Zhang 2019 (CellMarker), Franzen 2019 (PanglaoDB).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + statistical [Salmon EM transcript quantification + marker-panel argmax] | nonlinear [Seurat / Scanpy clustering then label-transfer] | linear-operator + deep neural [scVI variational, scANVI, scPhere]
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-413-like sequence-alignment + transcript-quantification bound. Misclassification probability bound: Pr(cell_type_hat != cell_type_true) <= C_thresh * |expression_z_score_error| / d(expression, marker_threshold).
C.complexity: O(N_reads * log(N_genes)) for alignment; O(N_cells * N_genes) for normalization + marker scoring; total alignment-dominated

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original framing: 'Cluster scRNA-seq cells and assign types' (would be data_driven_statistical Seurat / Scanpy clustering pipeline). Option-3 recast: 'Recover per-cell gene-expression vector via RNA-seq alignment + EM transcript quantification; apply CellMarker / PanglaoDB / Tabula Sapiens marker-panel scoring per cell'. Feasible because gene expression has a well-defined sampling forward model and marker panels are deterministic functions of expression vectors.

joint_well_posedness_references: Trapnell C et al. (2014) The dynamics and regulators of cell fate decisions are revealed by pseudotemporal ordering of single cells. Nat Biotechnol 32(4):381-386.
Macosko EZ et al. (2015) Highly parallel genome-wide expression profiling of individual cells using nanoliter droplets (Drop-seq). Cell 161(5):1202-1214.
Stuart T, Butler A et al. (2019) Comprehensive integration of single-cell data (Seurat v3). Cell 177(7):1888-1902.
Zhang X et al. (2019) CellMarker: a manually curated resource of cell markers in human and mouse. Nucleic Acids Res 47(D1):D721-D728.
Franzen O, Gan LM, Bjorkegren JLM (2019) PanglaoDB: a web server for exploration of mouse and human single-cell RNA sequencing data. Database 2019:baz046.
Tabula Sapiens Consortium (2022) The Tabula Sapiens: A multiple-organ, single-cell transcriptomic atlas of humans. Science 376(6594):eabl4896.
Lopez R, Regier J, Cole MB, Jordan MI, Yosef N (2018) Deep generative modeling for single-cell transcriptomics (scVI). Nat Methods 15(12):1053-1058.

distinctness_audit.advisory_label: Distinct (sibling-physics-core PWDR; future dedicated RNA-seq quantification core recommended)
distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-413 HMM Sequence Alignment uses HMM emission and transition probabilities for sequence-alignment problems. L1-520 RNA-seq cell-typing recovers transcript abundance via EM / variational Bayes (different forward model) and applies marker-panel categorical classification. Sibling computational-biology pattern but distinct primitives; d_principle ~ 0.55. Future work: author dedicated RNA-seq transcript-quantification analytical core.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Universal in single-cell biology, immunology, oncology (TIL profiling, immunotherapy response prediction), developmental biology (cell-fate trajectory analysis), and emerging clinical diagnostics (HCA cell census, BICCN brain atlas). 10x Genomics Chromium and BD Rhapsody dominate commercial single-cell platforms. UTSW Children's Medical Center Research Institute and Simmons Cancer Center run single-cell pipelines. Reference solvers: Salmon, kallisto, STAR (alignment); Seurat, Scanpy (analysis); CellMarker / PanglaoDB / Tabula Sapiens (marker panels). PWDR formulation gives physics-grounded explainability: cell types derive from explicit marker-gene expression thresholds tied to community taxonomies, not black-box clustering.
